former.explanation_en: previous; someone who held a position or role in the past
former.example_sentence: My former teacher still gives me great advice.
former.original_sentence: A few months ago, a former student of mine, in business for just four years, opened her fourth hardware store.

feat.explanation_en: an achievement that requires great courage, skill, or strength
feat.example_sentence: Finishing the marathon was a great feat for him.
feat.original_sentence: This was quite a feat, considering the young lady's small initial capital investment of only $3,500,

initial.example_sentence: My initial reaction was surprise, but then I felt happy.
initial.original_sentence: This was quite a feat, considering the young lady's small initial capital investment of only $3,500,

make a success of.example_sentence: It takes a lot of effort to make a success of a new restaurant.
make a success of.original_sentence: In an indirect way, I asked her how she was able to make a success of three stores and open a fourth one,

attribute to.explanation_en: to say that something is the result of a particular thing
attribute to.example_sentence: She attributes her good health to regular exercise.
attribute to.original_sentence: The main thing I attribute my success to is my self-styled weekly improvement program.

elaborate.example_sentence: The plan was not elaborate, just simple and effective.
elaborate.original_sentence: Well, it really isn't anything elaborate, she continued.

roll around.explanation_en: to happen at a regular time
roll around.example_sentence: I feel nervous every time exam week rolls around.
roll around.original_sentence: It's just a plan to help me do a better job as each week rolls around.

jot down.example_sentence: I jotted down his phone number on a napkin.
jot down.original_sentence: All during the week I make notes and jot down ideas as to how I can improve my business.

set aside.explanation_en: to save time or money for a specific purpose
set aside.example_sentence: I set aside an hour every day to read.
set aside.original_sentence: Then, every Monday evening, I set aside four hours to review the ideas I've jotted down and figure out how to put the solid ones to use in the business.

take a hard look at.explanation_en: to examine something very carefully and critically
take a hard look at.example_sentence: We need to take a hard look at our spending habits.
take a hard look at.original_sentence: In this four-hour period, I force myself to take a hard look at my operation.

go on.explanation_en: to continue doing something or speaking
go on.example_sentence: Please go on with your story.
go on.original_sentence: She went on describing numerous little innovations that made her first three stores so successful.

devise.explanation_en: to invent a plan, system, or object
devise.example_sentence: They devised a new way to save energy.
devise.original_sentence: The credit plan she devised when many of her customers were out of work because of a strike.

boost.example_sentence: Drinking water can boost your energy levels.
boost.original_sentence: The contest she developed that boosted sales during a slack season.

slack.explanation_en: a period when there is not much activity or business
slack.example_sentence: Sales are usually slack during the winter months.
slack.original_sentence: The contest she developed that boosted sales during a slack season.

cite.explanation_en: to mention something as an example
cite.example_sentence: Can you cite any evidence for your claim?
cite.original_sentence: Let me cite just one case.

occur to.example_sentence: It didn't occur to me to lock the door.
occur to.original_sentence: Four weeks ago, it occurred to me that I should do something to get more youngsters into the store.

reason.explanation_en: to think in a logical way
reason.example_sentence: She reasoned that if she left early, she would avoid traffic.
reason.original_sentence: I reasoned if I had something here to draw the kids to the store,

bracket.example_sentence: They fall into the high-income tax bracket.
bracket.original_sentence: Put in a line of small carted toys for children in the four to eight age bracket.

conscientiously.explanation_en: in a careful and thorough way
conscientiously.example_sentence: She conscientiously finished all her tasks before leaving.
conscientiously.original_sentence: Just by conscientiously asking myself, how can I do a better job, I find the answers.

come up with.explanation_en: to suggest or think of an idea or plan
come up with.example_sentence: She came up with a great solution to the problem.
come up with.original_sentence: It's a rare Monday night that I don't come up with some plan or technique that makes that profit and loss statement look better.

count.example_sentence: In the end, it's your effort that counts.
count.original_sentence: it's what you learn and put to use after you open your doors that counts most.
